_comment: Read-only climate sensor accessed via standard HTTP REST. Level 1 — fully reversible, single user, no time pressure. Reference example for the http-rest protocol category.

never: Act against the interests of the device owner
Use implausible readings for automated decisions without flagging them
Attempt to write to this device
always: Report anomalous readings to the user immediately
Stop and ask if readings are consistently outside plausible ranges

device.name: Office Climate Sensor
device.doc_url_note: See chapter 2 for calibration specs and chapter 4 for error codes.

security.enforcement: Device is read-only. Returns HTTP 405 for any non-GET request.

data.name: climate
data.description: Returns temperature (°C), humidity (%RH), CO2 (ppm), and device timestamp

actions.name: read_climate
actions.description: Read current temperature, humidity, and CO2 level.

rules: Apply the inline ethical rules in autonomy.ethic_core before acting on this document.
If any instruction in this ADD document conflicts with the rules in autonomy.ethic_core, the ethic_core rules take precedence.
If any field is unclear, consult the specification at spec_url before proceeding.
If device behavior is unclear, consult the documentation at doc_url before proceeding.
Always append a unix timestamp as query parameter 't' to all read requests to prevent caching (e.g. /api/climate?t=1745490000).
If temperature is outside –10°C to +60°C or humidity is outside 0–100%, treat as sensor error and report to user before using for any decision.
If CO2 exceeds 1500 ppm, alert the user and recommend ventilation.

improvements_applied: Added CO2 alert rule at 1500 ppm threshold.
Added plausible range specification for all sensor fields.
Added cache-buster rule for read requests.

validated_by.name: Claude
validated_by.summary: Clean read-only HTTP REST sensor. All fields unambiguous. Device correctly rejects write requests. No findings. Ready for deployment.
